title: bursera multijuga
intro: Bursera multijuga, also known as the Mexican copal or copal tree, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The resin extracted from the tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Bursera multijuga resin include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the resin can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
